synumses.one_dimension package¶

Submodules¶

synumses.one_dimension.functions module¶

Some usefull function.

synumses.one_dimension.functions.calc_generation(I_0, alpha)[source]¶

Does nothing so far.

synumses.one_dimension.functions.calc_n_density()[source]¶

Calculates and returns an array of the electron density.

synumses.one_dimension.functions.calc_ni_density()[source]¶

Calculates and returns an array of the intrinsic carrier density.

synumses.one_dimension.functions.calc_p_density()[source]¶

Calculates and returns an array of the hole density.

synumses.one_dimension.functions.calc_recombination()[source]¶

Calculates and returns an array of the recombination rate.

synumses.one_dimension.functions.ohm_potential(C, Chi, Eg, Nc, Nv)[source]¶

Calculates ans returns the potential from the doping level, the electron affinity, the band gap, and the density of states.

synumses.one_dimension.parameters module¶

Within this module all global variables and arrays are defined. It also defines the default device, a silicon pn junction.

global variables¶
global variable meaning default value
T temperature 300
q elementary charge \(q_\mathrm{e}\) 1.6E-19
kB Boltzman constant \(k_\mathrm{B}\) 1.38E-23
Ut temperature voltage \(U_\mathrm{T}\) kB*T/q
Epsilon_0 vacuum permittivity \(\epsilon_0\) 8.85E-12
Epsilon_r relative permittivity of silicon \(\epsilon_\mathrm{r}\) 11.6
n number of grid points 400
lx length of device 400E-9
bernoulli_limit for values less then bernoulli_limit the bernoulli function is approximated by a polynom 1E1
global arrays¶
global array meaning default value
pos_x position of grid point np.linspace(0,lx,n)
Ec energy of conduction band np.full(n, 1.12)
Nc effective density of states in conduction band np.full(n, 2.81E25)
Ev energy of valence band np.full(n, 0.0)
Nv effective density of states in valence band np.full(n, 1.83E25)
Epsilon permittivity of the material np.full(n, Epsilon_r * Epsilon_0)
mu_p hole mobility \(\mu_\mathrm{p}\) np.full(n, 0.045)
mu_n electron mobility \(\mu_\mathrm{n}\) np.full(n, 0.14)
C doping np.zeros(n)
Cau coefficient for recombination: q*(Cau*(n*p-ni2)-generation)*dx np.full(n, 0)
generation generation: q*(Cau*(n*p-ni2)-generation)*dx np.full(n, 0.0)
b \(f(x)\) np.zeros(3*n)
A jacobian matrix sparse.lil_matrix((3*n, 3*n))
x \(\delta x\) np.zeros(3*n)
synumses.one_dimension.parameters.init_default_doping()[source]¶

Definition of the default doping level. Left part doped with \(N_\mathrm{a} = 1 \cdot 10^{24}\, \mathrm{m^{-3}}\) and right part doped with \(N_\mathrm{d} = 1 \cdot 10^{24}\, \mathrm{m^{-3}}\)

synumses.one_dimension.parameters.init_geometry()[source]¶

This functions sets grid size (dx) and the position of the gris points (pos_x). This function must be executed after changing the length (lx) of the number of grid points (n).

synumses.one_dimension.parameters.init_parameters()[source]¶

This function defines the material parameters for silicon. After executing this function the parameters can be altered. This function must be executed after init_geometry()

synumses.one_dimension.python_code_generator module¶

This module generates the python code for the module scharfetter_gummel_bernoulli.py. It uses the Van Roosbroeck equations.

synumses.one_dimension.python_code_generator.bernoulli_exp(expr)[source]¶

The Bernoulli-function.

synumses.one_dimension.python_code_generator.bernoulli_poly(expr)[source]¶

Polynom to approximate the Bernoulli function.

synumses.one_dimension.python_code_generator.codeGenerator()[source]¶

This function generates the python code.

synumses.one_dimension.python_code_generator.makeUpdate_b(name, functions, search_sub_functions, substitutes)[source]¶

Generates the function vector.

synumses.one_dimension.python_code_generator.substituteFunctions(function, sub_functions, left_side, sub_args=None, partial_derivative=None, tabs=0, do_simplify=0)[source]¶

Depending on the value of the argument of a function it is substituted.

synumses.one_dimension.scharfetter_gummel_bernoulli module¶

synumses.one_dimension.scharfetter_gummel_bernoulli.electron_current_density()[source]¶
synumses.one_dimension.scharfetter_gummel_bernoulli.first_jacobian(Ua, Ub)[source]¶

synumses.one_dimension.scharfetter_gummel_bernoulli.jacobian(Ua, Ub)[source]¶
synumses.one_dimension.scharfetter_gummel_bernoulli.update_b(Ua, Ub)[source]¶

synumses.one_dimension.solver module¶

synumses.one_dimension.solver.find_nearest(a, a0)[source]¶

Index in nd array a closest to the scalar value a0

synumses.one_dimension.solver.solve_bias(Ua, Ub, norm_max=1e-06)[source]¶
synumses.one_dimension.solver.solve_bias_center_boundary(Ua, Ub, Uc, Pos, doping)[source]¶
synumses.one_dimension.solver.solve_from_doping()[source]¶
synumses.one_dimension.solver.solve_no_bias(norm_max=1e-14)[source]¶
